violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2026
2-door reach-in remains in poor repair. Unit has been placed out of service. Equipment must remain in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2026
2-door reach-in is not in good repair. It is currently not in use. Service call has been placed, and repair is in process. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2025

2-door reach-in was observed held at 52 degrees Fahrenheit. Foods in this unit observed with temperatures of 49 - 52 degrees F. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Service call placed. Voluntary disposal during inspection. No TCS foods are to be stored in this unit until it is maintaining a consistent temperature at/below 41 degrees F. New Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2025

Deli meat was observed not marked with the date of preparation/slicing. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared/sliced in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation/slicing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2025

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2025

A couple of the hand-washing sinks in the kitchen were lacking paper towels. Paper towels must be available at all times for proper hand-washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer, can opener) were observed with an accumulation of dried food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2025

Thermometer is lacking in the walk-in cooler. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2025

Individual cutting boards are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 03/14/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the 2-door lowboy gaskets have an accumulation of dust, dirt, black residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2025
Prep unit #1 is not in good repair. It is not currently functioning. Service has been contacted. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of refrigeration gaskets and the commercial mixer have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2024
Spoon observed in one of the hand-washing sinks. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, cooking utensils) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of some equipment (prep unit, mixer, dishwasher) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2024

Ambient temperature of walk-in cooler observed at 43 degrees Fahrenheit. Foods in this unit observed with temperatures of 41 degrees F. Ice build-up observed on the condenser. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. HVAC called for service. All TCS foods relocated to the school cafeteria walk-in cooler. No TCS foods to be stored in this unit until it has been serviced and is maintaining a consistent temperature at/below 41 degrees F. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2023

Thermometer is lacking in the walk-in refrigeration unit. All cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one internal thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the commercial mixer have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2023
Food and beverage debris observed in the hand-washing sinks. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/10/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (commercial mixers, slicer, knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/10/2023

Several small cutting boards are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2023

The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. Thermo labels or temperature measuring device must be available to monitor proper sanitizing temperature. New Violation. Correct By: 05/24/2023
